topic: Setting Up Admission Team Hierarchy and Data Visibility
summary: This topic outlines how to establish hierarchical admission teams with designated team leads and members, and how this structure influences data visibility among counselors and team leads.
steps: If a team structure is not required (e.g., a single senior person with multiple counselors), delete any existing teams and save the configuration.
To create a new team: Click 'Add Team', provide a team name (e.g., 'Domestic Team', 'External Team').
Assign a 'Team Lead' to the newly created team.
Add 'Members' to the team by selecting counselors from the available list.
Click 'Save' to finalize the team setup.

To view team members' data on the Admission Leads page: Filter by the Team Lead, then toggle 'Show Member Details' ON. This will display all leads belonging to the team lead and their team members combined.
business_rules: A Team Lead can view the lead data of all members within their assigned team.
Individual team members cannot view the lead data of other members within the same team.
Team Leads generally cannot view the lead data of other Team Leads' teams.
If a counselor is a member of two different teams, both respective Team Leads will be able to view that common member's lead data.
important_notes: Team structures are particularly beneficial for organizations with distinct outreach or inside admission teams.
The 'Show Member Details' filter is essential for Team Leads to monitor their team's collective performance and lead pipeline.
questions: How do I set up an admission team with a team lead and members?
How does data visibility work within an admission team?
Can a team lead see the leads of other teams?
What happens if a counselor is part of two different admission teams?
How do I view my team members' lead data on the admission leads page?
Where can I configure admission team structures?
